---
title: "Inside Toronto's Shelter System"
subtitle: "Monthly flow data from the City of Toronto, with analysis and findings"
---

Toronto publishes monthly data on its taxed shelter system, and the
City's own dashboards do a good job presenting the headline numbers.
This page goes a step further: interactive filters on every chart, a
clear note on what the data captures and what it leaves out, and
findings that usually stay buried in technical documentation.

The data covers every month from January 2018 to the most recent
published month, sourced directly from the [City of Toronto's Shelter
System Flow dataset](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/toronto-shelter-system-flow/).
New data loads automatically each month, usually around the 15th,
after the City publishes the prior month's figures.

## How to read this data

These numbers reflect the shelter *system*, not all homelessness. The
data captures people who used a City-funded overnight service at
least once in the past three months. It doesn't include people
sleeping outside, in shelters that aren't City-funded, or staying
temporarily with friends or family. The City [estimates](https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-system-flow-data/)
that roughly 18% of people experiencing absolute homelessness in
Toronto aren't reflected in these numbers. Trends here reflect
changes in the shelter system specifically — not in total homelessness
in the city.

## Key terms

The dashboards use a few terms that have specific meanings worth
knowing before you read the charts.

**Actively homeless.** A person who has used City-funded shelter
services at least once in the past three months and hasn't been
recorded as moving to permanent housing. This is the headline number
on every dashboard. Because it looks back three months, the count
includes people who may not be in shelter on any given night.

**Chronic homelessness.** The federal definition: someone who has
spent at least 180 nights in shelter over the past year, or at least
546 nights over the past three years. A person can meet this
definition while still actively in the shelter system — they don't
need to have left and returned.

**Inflow categories** (people entering the shelter system this month):

- **Newly Identified.** People entering the shelter system for the
  first time. One exception for the "Chronic" group: in that row,
  this column counts people who became chronically homeless during
  the reporting month, regardless of how long they'd already been
  using the shelter system.
- **Returned from Permanent Housing.** People who previously moved
  to permanent housing and have come back to the shelter system.
- **Returned to Shelter.** People who were in the system, didn't
  use it for at least three months, and have now returned.

**Outflow categories** (people leaving the shelter system this month):

- **Moved to Permanent Housing.** People who left the shelter system
  for permanent housing.
- **Became Inactive.** People who haven't used shelter services in
  the past three months, including the reporting month.

Definitions are based on the [City of Toronto's Shelter System Flow
Data page](https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-system-flow-data/).

[Read all data findings →](about-the-data.qmd)

## The dashboards

The three tabs below show different views of the data, mirroring the
City's own dashboards but with full interactive filtering on every
chart.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Monthly Snapshot

A point-in-time view of the shelter system for any month from January
2018 forward. Use the date filter at the top of the dashboard to
change months.

<iframe
  src="https://g88e375ebfb8a61-bonquery.adb.ca-toronto-1.oraclecloudapps.com/ords/r/shelter/toronto-shelter-analytics/monthly-snapshot"
  width="100%"
  height="900"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

### Year-to-Date Comparison

Compares the current year's cumulative figures against the same period
the prior year. Useful for seeing whether the system is on track to
exceed, match, or fall below last year.

<iframe
  src="https://g88e375ebfb8a61-bonquery.adb.ca-toronto-1.oraclecloudapps.com/ords/r/shelter/toronto-shelter-analytics/ytd-comparison"
  width="100%"
  height="900"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

### Historical Trends

Multi-year time series for the headline metrics, with filters for
population group, year range, and month. Useful for spotting long-term
patterns or seasonal trends.

<iframe
  src="https://g88e375ebfb8a61-bonquery.adb.ca-toronto-1.oraclecloudapps.com/ords/r/shelter/toronto-shelter-analytics/historical-trends"
  width="100%"
  height="900"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

:::

## Data source

All data comes from the City of Toronto's [Shelter System Flow
dataset](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/toronto-shelter-system-flow/),
published monthly on the City's open data portal under the
[Open Government Licence — Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-license/).
